post.summary: Set pubsub configuration for a user
post.description:
Pubsub is the method of configuring what events you want delivered to [HTTPS / SNS Callbacks](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/real-time-device-updates/#https-sns-callbacks) by SmartHQ. This endpoint sets the pubsub configuration for the user the [access token](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/authorization/#oauth-20-tokens) is tied to.

To learn about this endpoints purpose and use case, please see [What is Pubsub?](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/real-time-device-updates/#what-is-pubsub) and the [overview description and table of Real Time Device Updates](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/real-time-device-updates/#overview)

This endpoint is to set the account level configuration (for the user itself). To see the values that can be sent through the requests and their descriptions see: [Event Configuration Messages](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/real-time-device-updates/#event-configuration-messages) and specifically "Account-Level Configuration Request below that."

Note: if optional parameters are omitted, they will default to false.

get.summary: Get account-level pubsub configuration for a user
get.description:
Returns the account-level pubsub configuration for a user the [access token](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/authorization/#oauth-20-tokens) is tied to.

To learn about this endpoints purpose and use case, please see [What is Pubsub?](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/real-time-device-updates/#what-is-pubsub) and the [overview description and table of Real Time Device Updates](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/real-time-device-updates/#overview)

delete.summary: Disable pubsub for a user and all their devices
delete.description:
Disable pubsub for a user and all their devices.

The user that the pubsub configuration is disabled for is identified by the [access token](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/authorization/#oauth-20-tokens) used to make the request.

Note: deleting the pubsub configuration only affects the pubsub for your application and does not affect other applications.

post.summary: Set pubsub configuration for a device in user's account
post.description:
Sets the pubsub configuration for a device of a user that the [access token](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/authorization/#oauth-20-tokens) is tied to.

This endpoint is to set the device level configuration (for the device itself for a particular end user). To see the values that can be sent through the requests and their descriptions see: [Event Configuration Messages](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/real-time-device-updates/#event-configuration-messages) and specifically "Device-Level Configuration Request below that."

Note: if optional parameters are omitted, they will default to false.

Note: In order to subscribe to Device level changes, you must first enable pubsub at the User level. You don't have to enable User level subscriptions for services or presence but do need to enable pubsub.

get.summary: Get pubsub configuration for an individual device
get.description: Returns the pubsub configuration for an individual device.

delete.description: Remove pubsub configuration for a device.

204.description: Successfully removed the device pubsub configuration

get.description:
Returns the most recent [alert](https://docs.smarthq.com/data-model/overview/#alerts) report based on client permission, organization, and token policy along with queryparams.

**Default Behavior:**

When missing time parameters, the system applies default values:
- If before is specified without after: defaults after to 30 days prior
- If after is specified without before: defaults before to current date
- All existing validations still apply in both scenarios

**Filtering:**

Filtering can be applied by including query string parameters with the request. To match multiple values, join the values with a comma and negation with ^.

**Filter Parameters:** deviceId, alertType, deviceType, building, room, macAddress, serial, location, model, floor, unit

**Request Examples:**
- Basic request: GET /v2/alert/recent
- Last 10 days: ?after=10d
- Grouped fault alerts: ?after=30d&group=true&fault=true
- Relative date range: ?after=10d&before=5d
- Absolute time range: ?after=2024-05-01T05:31:41.720Z&before=2024-05-15T05:31:41.720Z

400.description: Invalid page size, invalid page number, invalid input

403.description: User or application does not have permission to view device or alert

parameters.name: after (relative)
parameters.description: Starting point of history data. Relative format: ^[\d]+[dhm]$ (Ex: 4d for 4 days, 10m for 10 minutes)

parameters.name: before (relative)
parameters.description: Ending point of history data. Relative format: ^[\d]+[dhm]$ (Ex: 4d for 4 days, 10m for 10 minutes)

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of appliances returned, default to 50

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Order data for max date on appliance, default to DESC

parameters.name: fault
parameters.description: Supported values are true OR false. If true it return alerts which are faults. If false it return alerts which are not faults. This is OPTIONAL. If this is not passed then it returns all alerts.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Supported values are true OR false. If true it returns group of deviceType and alertType with latest alertTime and deviceId. if false it returns the usual response.

get.summary: List devices in a user's account
get.description:
Gets the list of devices for an account. The account is determined by the [access token](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/authorization/#oauth-20-tokens) passed as the bearer token in the authorization header.

**Basic Usage:** For simple use cases or small device counts, call this endpoint without any parameters to get all devices.

**Advanced Usage:** This endpoint uses pagination and supports sorting & filtering options.

- Pagination: By default, it will return 50 devices but you can specify the number of devices per page using the perpage query string parameter in conjunction with the page query string parameter.

- Sorting: the list can be sorted alphabetically by nickname. By default, the sorting option is set to false, but it can be activated by specifying true in the query string parameters. The sorting order may be either desc or asc.

**How to Use Filtering:** To make use of filtering, scroll down to the 'Responses' section of this endpoint and select different examples. The examples should include 'Basic device list example' and 'Negative filter example' in addition to others. Under the JSON response itself (the section titled 'Example Value') will be a section titled, 'Example Description' which explains how to use each filtering option.

filteredByRoomNumber.description:
Devices can be filtered by a tag, for example 'roomNumber'

Example Request: `GET /v2/device?roomNumber=101`

Filtering can be applied to the device list by including query string parameters with the request. The valid filter parameters are listed below. To match multiple values, join the values with a comma.

Filter Parameters: 'deviceId', 'adapterId', 'deviceType', 'gatewayId', 'model', 'serial', 'version', 'manufacturer', 'brand', 'macAddress', 'updId', 'room', 'roomNumber', 'unit', 'floor', 'building', 'location', 'personality'

wildcardFilter.description:
Devices can be filtered by wildcard matching.

Example Request: `GET /v2/device?roomNumber=101&deviceType=cloud.smarthq.device.*oven`

More Information: Filtering also works with wildcard matching. Include one or more of the following characters to activate it:

`*` - match 0 or more characters

`?` - match exactly 1 character

his combines with negation, so you can add `^` at the beginning of the pattern to return everything except what matches the pattern

**Note:** At this time, wildcard matching cannot be used with multiple values. When your value contains one or more of the wildcard characters, any ',' will be ignored and treated as part of the literal value.

negationFilter.description:
Devices can be filtered by negation.

Example Request: `GET /v2/device?roomNumber=101&deviceType=^cloud.smarthq.device.room`

More Information: Filtering also works with negation to exclude certain values for any filter type by adding a ^ symbol before the filter value. To negate multiple values, you must add a ^ before each. Ex: deviceType=^foo,^bar

basicList.description:
A basic request without any filters applied. This API endpoint is called without any query parameters.

Example Request: `GET /v2/device`

401.description: Unauthorized. The request requires user authentication

403.description: Forbidden. Client does not have permission to view the devices

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort key - can be any filter parameter listed below or `nickname`. Optional.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Sort order - ascending or descending. Optional, defaults to ascending.

get.summary: Get the WebSocket endpoint URL for the user
get.description:
This endpoint returns a WebSocket URL that can used to recieve real time updates from SmartHQ.

The response's WebSocket URL pertains to a single end-user's SmartHQ account, which is defined by the [access token](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/authorization/#oauth-20-tokens).

See our [Real Time Device Updates Documentation](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/real-time-device-updates/) to understand the purpose of this endpoint and how to make use of it.

The response includes aggregations of outcomes for 'success', 'failed', 'unknown', and 'timeout'. The response will also include outcomes, which is a breakdown of all outcomes with a count for each.

get.description:
Returns the [alert](https://docs.smarthq.com/data-model/overview/#alerts) report based on the alert type, client permission, organization and token policy along with queryparams.

**Default Behavior:**

- After defaults after to 30 days
- Timeframe defaults to relative
- Before defaults to current date
- Page size defaults to 1
- Order defaults to DESC (descending)

***Query Parameters:**

**Request:**
```
GET /v2/alert/{alertType}/report HTTP/1.1
Host: client.mysmarthq.com
Authorization: Bearer 1/fFAGRNJru1FTz70EXAMPLE
```

**Request Example for last 120 days**
```
GET /v2/alert/cloud.smarthq.alert.fault.drainblock.lower/report?after=120d HTTP/1.1
Host: client.mysmarthq.com
Authorization: Bearer 1/fFAGRNJru1FTz70EXAMPLE
```

400.description: Invalid timeframe format, invalid timestamp, invalid page size, invalid page number

403.description: User or application does not have permission to view device or alert

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Amount of time to get the desired alert type, examples: 50d, 12h, 30m. Default to 30d

parameters.name: timeFrameType
parameters.description: The type of timeframe being requested. relative or absolute. Default to relative

parameters.name: after (absolute)
parameters.description: Starting point of history data. (Ex: 2022-07-01T08:27:39.671Z). Default to current date minus 30 days.

parameters.name: before (absolute)
parameters.description: Ending point of history data. (Ex: 2022-07-01T08:27:39.671Z). Default to current date.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of appliances returned, default to 50

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Order data for max date on appliance, default to DESC

parameters.name: deviceType
parameters.description: Field to filter by deviceType (Ex: cloud.smarthq.device.ptac)

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: Field to filter by location (Ex: lsogjwg)

Save the favorite to the account.

To see the options for what can be sent in this request see [Saving a new favorite (using POST /v2/favorite)](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/favorites/#saving-a-new-favorite-using-post-v2favorite) and the entire page in general for context.

403.description: Forbidden: User does not have permission to save favorites

get.description:
Get the list of favorites for the account. Favorites will be sorted by the order property.

See [Favorites Documentation](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/favorites/) to learn more.

Filtering is optional. Filtering can be applied to the favorite list by including a query parameter deviceType with the request. Supports only one deviceType at a time.

403.description: Forbidden: User does not have permission to access favorites

parameters.name: deviceType
parameters.description: (Optional) Filter by device type with prefix 'cloud.smarthq.device' such as 'cloud.smarthq.device.smoker'.

post.description:
Manage all tags for a gateway.

Multiple tags can be updated in a single request. Gateway tags cannot be managed individually. It is recommended that the client make a request to list the gateway tags, then make modifications to that list and post the modified list to this endpoint. If a tag was previously assigned to a gateway and then omitted in an update, it will be removed from the gateway and subsequently all devices on the gateway.

**Permissions** If a user does not have permission to manage a certain tag, it will be omitted from the tag updates. If there was a previous value existing for the tag which was omitted, it will remain unmodified. The response will contain the list of tags which were updated.

400.description: Bad request: duplicate tag name specified

403.description: User or application does not have required permissions

parameters.name: gatewayId
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the gateway

get.description: Returns all defined tags for a gateway.

403.description: User or application does not have required permissions

parameters.name: gatewayId
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the gateway

delete.description: Remove all tags for a gateway. If a user does not have permission to manage a certain tag, it will be omitted from the removals.

403.description: User or application does not have required permissions

parameters.name: gatewayId
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the gateway

parameters.name: fileId

post.description:
This endpoint allows for querying and aggregation of a device's service state history.

This endpoint also allows downloading raw history data in Excel format with a TTL of 60mins (set output to raw). For each data object there will be a sheet created in the same file for the downloaded history data. There is no limitation on the data size.

**Parameters:**
- **kind:** string - device#history
- **output:** string - The format of the output. Currently line and raw is supported.
- **data:** object - The data being requested. Currently only supports a type of service.
- **timeframe:** object - The timeframe of history being requested. Currently only supports type of relative.

**The data object:**
- **type:** string - The type of data being requested. Currently only supports service.
- **metrics:** array - The names of the metrics to plot for the service.
- **serviceType:** string - (required for type of service) The serviceType.
- **domainType:** string - (required for type of service) The domainType.
- **serviceDeviceType:** string - (required for type of service) The serviceDeviceType.

**The timeframe object:**
- **type:** string - The type of timeframe being requested. relative or absolute.
- **after:** string - Starting point of history data. Relative format: `^[\d]+[d
- **after (absolute):** string - Starting point of history data. (Ex: 2022-07-01T08:27:39.671Z)
- **before (absolute):** string - Ending point of history data. (Ex: 2022-07-01T08:27:39.671Z)
- **interval:** string - Interval for data. Relative format: `^[\d]+[d

**Request Body for relative time range:**
```json
{
  "kind": "device#history",
  "output": "line",
  "data": [
    {
      "type": "service",
      "serviceType": "cloud.smarthq.service.temperature",
      "domainType": "cloud.smarthq.domain.indoor.ambient",
      "serviceDeviceType": "cloud.smarthq.device.ptac",
      "metrics": [
        "fahrenheit"
      ]
    },
    {
      "type": "service",
      "serviceType": "cloud.smarthq.service.temperature",
      "domainType": "cloud.smarthq.domain.outdoor.ambient",
      "serviceDeviceType": "cloud.smarthq.device.ptac",
      "metrics": [
        "fahrenheit"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "timeframe": {
    "type": "relative",
    "after": "1d",
    "interval": "4h"
  }
}
```

**Request Body for absolute time range:**
```json
{
  "kind": "device#history",
  "output": "line",
  "data": [
    {
      "type": "service",
      "serviceType": "cloud.smarthq.service.temperature",
      "domainType": "cloud.smarthq.domain.indoor.ambient",
      "serviceDeviceType": "cloud.smarthq.device.ptac",
      "metrics": [
        "fahrenheit"
      ]
    },
    {
      "type": "service",
      "serviceType": "cloud.smarthq.service.temperature",
      "domainType": "cloud.smarthq.domain.outdoor.ambient",
      "serviceDeviceType": "cloud.smarthq.device.ptac",
      "metrics": [
        "fahrenheit"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "timeframe": {
    "type": "absolute",
    "after": "2022-07-01T08:27:39.671Z",
    "before": "2022-07-28T08:27:39.671Z",
    "interval": "12h"
  }
}
```

**Request Body for absolute time range Raw data:**
```json
{
  "kind": "device#history",
  "output": "raw",
  "data": [
    {
      "type": "service",
      "serviceType": "cloud.smarthq.service.temperature",
      "domainType": "cloud.smarthq.domain.indoor.ambient",
      "serviceDeviceType": "cloud.smarthq.device.ptac",
      "metrics": [
        "fahrenheit"
      ]
    },
    {
      "type": "service",
      "serviceType": "cloud.smarthq.service.temperature",
      "domainType": "cloud.smarthq.domain.outdoor.ambient",
      "serviceDeviceType": "cloud.smarthq.device.ptac",
      "metrics": [
        "fahrenheit"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "timeframe": {
    "type": "absolute",
    "after": "2022-07-01T08:27:39.671Z",
    "before": "2022-07-28T08:27:39.671Z"
  }
}
```

400.description: Bad request, or number of data points too high (adjust time/interval)

403.description: Client does not have permission to device

post.description:
This endpoint enables you to control a device. See [this page to learn about commands](https://docs.smarthq.com/data-model/overview/#commands). See our [Sending a Command Tutorial](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/tutorial/#3-send-a-command) to learn how to use them.

**Parameters:**
- kind (string) - service#command
- deviceId (string) - The ID of the device to command.
- serviceType (string) - The service type to command.
- domainType (string) - The domain of the service to command.
- serviceDeviceType (string) - The device type of the service to command (not necessarily the device type of the appliance)
- command (object) - The command with parameters.
- successOnNoDeviceMatches (boolean) - If true changes API behavior to not return a 404 if no devices are matched for the command.

200.description: Command was successfully processed. Check the `success` and `outcome` fields in the response to evaluate whether it ran successfully or if an error occurred

412.description: Gateway or device is offline, or service state is unknown

403.description: Client does not have permission

get.description:
List the commands (with outcomes) issued across multiple devices in an account.

**Pagination:** This endpoint is paginated, and the page can be selected by specifying a query string parameter 'page'. By default, it will return 50 commands but you can specify the number of commands per page using the 'perpage' query string parameter in conjunction with the 'page' query string parameter.

**Filtering:**
Filtering can be applied to the command list by including query string parameters with the request. The valid filter parameters are listed below. To match multiple values, join the values with a comma. Filtering is optional.

Gets the service history for a single service.

**Query Params:** (All optional)
- pageSize - The size of the service history to be returned (should not be 0 or negative or more than 200)
- pageNumber - The page of the service history to be returned (should not be 0 or negative)
- timeFrame - The time stamp from when the history should be returned (it should be in the format of before:UTC Time Stamp or after:UTC Time Stamp. ex:before:2020-02-05T15:40:59.336Z)

**Request Example:**

GET /v2/device/326ec2.../service/5f9f17.../history?pageSize=200&timeFrame=before:2020-02-22T08:27:39.671Z

400.description: If PageSize is more than 200, or If timeFrame is not in correct format (should have before or after and timestamp)

403.description: Client does not have permission to view the device

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the device.

parameters.name: serviceId
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the service.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The size of the service history to be returned (should not be 0 or negative or more than 200)

parameters.name: pageNumber
parameters.description: The page of the service history to be returned (should not be 0 or negative)

parameters.name: timeFrame
parameters.description: The time stamp from when the history should be returned (it should be in the format of before:UTC Time Stamp or after:UTC Time Stamp. ex:before:2020-02-05T15:40:59.336Z)

**Optional Tag Attributes:** The device response may include additional metadata fields that provide extra information about the device's physical properties and location. These fields are optional - they will only appear in the response if they have been configured or are available for that specific device: model, serial, version, manufacturer, brand, macAddress, nickname, icon, room, roomNumber, unit, floor, building, location

get.description:
Gets the list of JSON Schemas used by this API. The schema is used to validate the structure of an API request to SmartHQ.

Once a schema is returned by this API, such as...

{

"name": "client.post.devicePubsub",

"url": "https://client-int.mysmarthq.com/v2/schema/client.post.devicePubsub.json"

}

 you can call 'GET /v2/schema/{schemaName}' (in this API spec) with the schema's name, such as GET /v2/schema/client.post.devicePubsub.json

get.summary: Get the number of devices in a user's account
get.description:
Gets the count of devices for an account based the user's permissions. The actual account may have more devices that are not shown in the response if access to those devices is restricted.

**Filtering:** Filtering can be applied to the device list by including query string parameters with the request. The valid filter parameters are listed below. To match multiple values, join the values with a comma.

**Aggregation:**
Aggregation can be done by using the query param groupBy where any of the above filtering keys could be passed.

Example Request with Filtering and Aggregation: `GET /v2/device/count?groupBy=deviceType&location=liimnhj`

200.description: Successful response containing device count statistics

parameters.name: groupBy
parameters.description: Field to be used by groupBy. Can be any of the filter parameters.

sampleRequest.summary: Sample request for device pubsub schema test

post.description:
Tests that payload validates with a JSON Schema used by the API. For example, if GET /v2/schema returns, such as...

{

"name": "client.post.devicePubsub",

"url": "https://client-int.mysmarthq.com/v2/schema/client.post.devicePubsub.json"

}

 you could verify a request in that schema by sending the reqest in this example to POST /v2/schema/client.post.devicePubsub.json

Note: The URL endpoint should include ".json" at the end of the schema's name.

200.description: The payload successfully validates against the JSON schema

get.description: Gets the JSON Schema used by an API request. Example: GET /v2/schema/client.post.devicePubsub.json (Include the .json at the end of the schema's name from the /v2/schema reponse)

parameters.name: schemaName
parameters.description: The unique name of the schema to retrieve.

post.summary: Query a device's calculated service state history with optional timezone
post.description:
This endpoint is almost exactly the same as the Device History endpoint, but it allows for the calculation of additional metrics based on the data returned and introduces an optional timezone parameter.

The number of data points returned is limited to 96; i.e., 1d with 15m interval would be 96, anything greater returns 400.

For requests with output set to line, the labels array containing the timestamps for each data point will be converted back to the timezone specified in the request. Requests with output set to raw will not have output be converted back to the requested timezone, they will remain in UTC. The request can still include a timezone and the timeframe will be converted.

Timeframe contains type. The absolute type allows to specify date ranges with after and before parameters containing timestamps. The relative type allows to specify date ranges with after and before parameters containing number of days, hours or minutes in the past. monthly, daily and hourly types return values for truncated timestamps and range specified by after parameter.

**Parameters:**
- **kind:** string - device#calculatedhistory
- **output:** string - The format of the output. Currently line and raw is supported.
- **data:** object - The data being requested. Currently only supports a type of service.
- **timeframe:** object - The timeframe of history being requested. Type absolute, relative, monthly, daily and hourly are supported.
- **timezone:** string - The timezone of the timeframe values. There should be one value in this format: America/New_York

400.description: Bad request, number of data points too high (adjust time/interval), no data points found for given timeframe

403.description: Client does not have permission to device

post.description: Gets a token to provision a new gateway to an account. This endpoint is used for the ['Adding External Devices' usecase](https://docs.smarthq.com/adding-external-devices/overview/). Token must be passed to an adapter to register new gateway using Device Adapter API.

400.description: JSONSchema Failed Validation. Missing post JSON body

401.description: Unauthorized. The request requires user authentication

post.description:
This will send a [command](https://docs.smarthq.com/data-model/overview/#commands) to multiple devices matching provided filtering criteria. To learn about sending commands, see our [Send a Command Documentation](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/tutorial/)

**Request Body Parameters:**

See the request body section below. This is where the actual command is sent.

- kind (string) - service#commands
- serviceType (string) - The service type to command.
- domainType (string) - The domain of the service to command.
- serviceDeviceType (string) - The device type of the service to command (not necessarily the device type of the appliance)
- command (object) - The command with parameters.

**Filter Parameters:**

Filtering is completely optional. Filters are passed as query string parameters to the endpoint. At least one device must match the provided criteria. If matches are found, the response will include a correlationId which can be used to query for the outcomes of the commands on individual devices and total indicating how many devices the command will be sent to. The same conditions for an individual command must be met.
- deviceId
- deviceType
- adapterId
- gatewayId
- model
- serial
- version
- manufacturer
- brand
- macAddress
- updId
- room
- roomNumber
- unit
- floor
- building
- location
- personality

Filtering Example: POST /v2/commands?deviceType=cloud.smarthq.device.refrigerator (with the request body still sent)

200.description: Command was successfully processed. Check the success and outcome fields in the response to evaluate whether it ran successfully or if an error occurred

403.description: Client does not have permission

404.description: No devices found matching filter criteria

List the batch commands issued in an account. This endpoint is paginated, and the page can be selected by specifying a query string parameter 'page'. By default, it will return 50 commands but you can specify the number of commands per page using the 'perpage' query string parameter in conjunction with the 'page' query string parameter.

**Filtering:**
Filtering is completely optional. Filtering can be applied to the command list by including query string parameters with the request. The valid filter parameters are listed below. To match multiple values, join the values with a comma.

The optional force query parameter can be provided with the value of "true" in order to communicate to the adapter/gateway that the device should more forcefully be removed. The specifics of how to interpret a force removal as opposed to a standard removal is left up to the implementation of the gateway.

Returns the alert history for an alert type on a device. The response includes timestamps: `alertTime` indicates when the alert was triggered, and `clearTime` indicates when the alert was cleared.

**Query String Parameter:**

Name | Value | Description
-----|-------|------------
status | string | Filter by the alert status. Available value(s): cleared and triggered. Default include both triggered and cleared.`

400.description: Invalid timeframe format, invalid timestamp, invalid page size

403.description: User or application does not have permission to view device or alert

get.description:
List the outcome(s) of an issued command. This endpoint is paginated, and the page can be selected by specifying a query string parameter page. By default, it will return 50 outcomes but you can specify the number of outcomes per page using the perpage query string parameter in conjunction with the page query string parameter.

**Filtering:**
Filtering is completely optional. Filtering can be applied to the command list by including query string parameters with the request. The valid filter parameters are listed below. To match multiple values, join the values with a comma.

parameters.name: correlationId
parameters.description: The correlation ID of the command.

get.description:
List the defined [alert](https://docs.smarthq.com/data-model/overview/#alerts) types for a device, including the last time each alert was triggered, as well as a count of both faults and alerts.

Also alertId has been included and it is a unique identifier for when an alert is triggered, and will change every time the alert is triggered. Alerts which have never been triggered or have been cleared will not have an alertId.

**Filtering:**

Filtering can be applied by including optional query string parameters with the request.

**Filter parameters:**

| Name | Value | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| status | string | Supported values are 'triggered\|cleared'. If 'triggered' it return alerts/faults which are triggered (The ones that have 'lastAlertTime' field). If 'cleared' it return alerts/faults which are marked with 'clear' = true. This is OPTIONAL. If this is not passed then it returns all alerts/faults. |
| fault | boolean | Supported values are 'true\|false'. If 'true' it return alerts which are faults. If 'false' it return alerts which are not faults. This is OPTIONAL. If this is not passed then it returns all alerts. |
| clear | boolean | Supported values are 'true\|false'. If 'true' it return actively cleared alerts and/or faults. If 'false' it return alerts and/or faults that have been cleared in the past. Default false. |

**Request:**
```
GET /v2/device/{deviceId}/alert HTTP/1.1
Host: client.mysmarthq.com
Authorization: Bearer 1/fFAGRNJru1FTz70EXAMPLE
```

**Request with status=triggered:**
```
GET /v2/device/{deviceId}/alert?status=triggered HTTP/1.1
Host: client.mysmarthq.com
Authorization: Bearer 1/fFAGRNJru1FTz70EXAMPLE
```

**Request with status=cleared:**
```
GET /v2/device/{deviceId}/alert?status=cleared HTTP/1.1
Host: client.mysmarthq.com
Authorization: Bearer 1/fFAGRNJru1FTz70EXAMPLE
```

**Request with clear=true:**
```
GET /v2/device/{deviceId}/alert?clear=true HTTP/1.1
Host: client.mysmarthq.com
Authorization: Bearer 1/fFAGRNJru1FTz70EXAMPLE
```

**Request with onlyTriggered=true:**
```
GET /v2/device/{deviceId}/alert?onlyTriggered=true HTTP/1.1
Host: client.mysmarthq.com
Authorization: Bearer 1/fFAGRNJru1FTz70EXAMPLE
```

**Request with onlyTriggered=true&fault=false:**
```
GET /v2/device/{deviceId}/alert?onlyTriggered=true&fault=false HTTP/1.1
Host: client.mysmarthq.com
Authorization: Bearer 1/fFAGRNJru1FTz70EXAMPLE
```

**Request with onlyTriggered=true&fault=true:**
```
GET /v2/device/{deviceId}/alert?onlyTriggered=true&fault=true HTTP/1.1
Host: client.mysmarthq.com
Authorization: Bearer 1/fFAGRNJru1FTz70EXAMPLE
```

403.description: User or application does not have permission to view device or alert

404.description: Device not found, or invalid alert type

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by alert status. Supported values are triggered|cleared (optional).

parameters.name: fault
parameters.description: Filter by fault type. If true return alerts which are faults. If false return alerts which are not faults (optional).

parameters.name: clear
parameters.description: Filter by clear status. If true return actively cleared alerts and/or faults. If false return alerts and/or faults that have been cleared in the past (optional).

parameters.name: onlyTriggered
parameters.description: Filter to only show triggered alerts (optional).

relativeTimeRangeWithFault.summary: Request with relative time range and fault filter

post.description:
Returns the [alert](https://docs.smarthq.com/data-model/overview/#alerts) report based on client permission and token policy along with queryparams.

**Parameters:**

| Name | Value | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| kind | string | alert#report |
| output | string | The format of the output. Currently only bar and line is supported. |
| timeframe | object | The timeframe of history being requested. supports the type of relative or absolute. |
| fault | boolean | Supported values are 'true OR false'. If 'true' it return alerts which are faults. If 'false' it return alerts which are not faults. This is OPTIONAL. If this is not passed then it returns all alerts. |

**The timeframe object:**

| Name | Value | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| type | string | The type of timeframe being requested. relative or absolute. |
| after | string | Starting point of history data. Relative format: '^[\d]+[d\|h\|m\|s]$' (Ex: '4d' for 4 days, '10m' for 10 minutes) |
| after (absolute) | string | Starting point of history data. (Ex: 2022-07-01T08:27:39.671Z) |
| before (absolute) | string | Ending point of history data. (Ex: 2022-07-01T08:27:39.671Z) |

**Query String Parameter:**

| Name | Value | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| pageSize | string | (Only raw output) Number of items returned, default to 10 |
| page | string | (Only raw output) Page number, default to 1 |
| deviceId | string | Filter items by deviceId |
| unit | string | Filter items by unit |

**Filtering:**

Filtering can be applied by including query string parameters with the request. The valid filter parameters are listed below. To match multiple values, join the values with a comma and negation with '^'.

Filter Parameters:
* 'alertType'
* 'deviceType'
* 'building'
* 'room'
* 'macAddress'
* 'serial'
* 'location'
* 'model'
* 'deviceId'
* 'unit'
* 'floor'

**Request:**
```
POST /v2/alert HTTP/1.1
Host: client.mysmarthq.com
Authorization: Bearer 1/fFAGRNJru1FTz70EXAMPLE
```

**Request Body for absolute time range:**
```json
{
  "kind": "alert#report",
  "output": "bar",
  "timeframe": {
    "type": "absolute",
    "after": "2022-12-01T00:30:00.000Z",
    "before": "2022-12-30T24:00:00.000Z"
  }
}
```

**Request Body for relative time range with filter fault:**
```json
{
  "kind": "alert#report",
  "output": "bar",
  "fault": true,
  "timeframe": {
    "type": "relative",
    "after": "30d"
  }
}
```

**Request Body for Raw output, grouped by deviceType:**
```json
{
  "kind": "alert#report",
  "output": "raw",
  "timeframe": {
    "type": "absolute",
    "after": "2022-12-01T00:30:00.000Z",
    "before": "2023-10-30T24:00:00.000Z"
  }
}
```

400.description: Invalid timeframe format, invalid timestamp, invalid page size

403.description: User or application does not have permission to view device or alert

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: (Only raw output) Number of items returned, default to 10

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: (Only raw output) Page number, default to 1

get.description:
Returns the count of number of active [alerts](https://docs.smarthq.com/data-model/overview/#alerts) during a given time frame, optionally grouped by a tag.

**Query Parameters:**

| Name | Value | Required | Description |
|------|-------|----------|-------------|
| after (relative) | string | No | Starting point of history data. Relative format: `^[\d]$` (Ex: `7d` for 7 days) |
| pageSize | string | No | Number of appliances returned, default to `50` |
| page | string | No | Page number, default to `1` |
| order | string | No | Order data for max date on appliance, default to `DESC` |
| fault | boolean | No | Supported values are `true OR false`. If `true` it return alerts which are faults. If `false` it return alerts which are not faults. This is OPTIONAL. If this is not passed then it returns all alerts. |
| groupBy | boolean | No | An ordered comma separated list of attributes to group by. Can be alertType, deviceType, adapterId, gatewayId or any of the tag values other than nickname. |

**Filtering:**

Filtering can be applied by including query string parameters with the request. The valid filter parameters are listed below. To match multiple values, join the values with a comma and negation with `^`.

Filter Parameters:
* deviceId
* alertType
* deviceType
* building
* room
* macAddress
* serial
* location
* model
* floor
* unit

**Request:**
```
GET /v2/alert/count HTTP/1.1
Host: client.mysmarthq.com
Authorization: Bearer 1/fFAGRNJru1FTz70EXAMPLE
```

**Request Example for no groupBy**
```
GET /v2/alert/count?after=10d HTTP/1.1
Host: client.mysmarthq.com
Authorization: Bearer 1/fFAGRNJru1FTz70EXAMPLE
```

**Request Example for groupBy and Filter List**
```
GET /v2/alert/count?after=30d?fault=true&groupBy=location&deviceType=cloud.smarthq.device.ptac,cloud.smarthq.device.vtac HTTP/1.1
Host: client.mysmarthq.com
Authorization: Bearer 1/fFAGRNJru1FTz70EXAMPLE
```

400.description: Invalid timeframe format, invalid timestamp, invalid page size, invalid page number

403.description: User or application does not have permission to view device or alert

parameters.name: after (relative)
parameters.description: Starting point of history data. Relative format: ^[\d]$ (Ex: 7d for 7 days)

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of appliances returned, default to 50

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Order data for max date on appliance, default to DESC

parameters.name: fault
parameters.description: Supported values are true OR false. If true it return alerts which are faults. If false it return alerts which are not faults. This is OPTIONAL. If this is not passed then it returns all alerts.

parameters.name: groupBy
parameters.description: An ordered comma separated list of attributes to group by. Can be alertType, deviceType, adapterId, gatewayId or any of the tag values other than nickname.

post.summary: Create or update a device tag
post.description: Sets the tag value for a given tag on a device. If the tag is set at the gateway level, the request will fail.

400.description: Bad request: tag names do not match, tag value is invalid

412.description: Tag is managed at the gateway level

403.description: User or application does not have permission to manage tags

get.description:
Returns the tag value for a given tag on a device.

**Valid Tag Names:** building, floor, location, nickname, room, roomNumber, unit

**Tag Value Format:** The tags above must match the pattern `^[\w\d\s\.',-:()]+$` (alphanumeric characters, spaces, and common punctuation). The exception is the nickname tag, which uses XSS sanitizing only.

404.description: Device not found, or value or tagName not found.

delete.summary: Remove a tag from a device.
delete.description: Remove a tag from a device.

403.description: User or application does not have permission to manage tags

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: ID of the device to remove

post.description: Submits device data, metrics, and a timeframe to calculate historical data in specified output format.

200.description: Successful calculation of device history data.

get.description: Gets the device settings for a single device.

403.description: User or application does not have permission to view device

tags.name: Device Information
tags.description: Endpoints that allow for device information or control

tags.name: Service Information
tags.description: Endpoints that return service details or query service state history

tags.name: Device Tags
tags.description: Endpoints that related to device tag information

tags.name: Alerts
tags.description: Endpoints related to alert reports and notifications

tags.name: File Download
tags.description: Endpoints that provide URL for file download

tags.name: Websocket
tags.description: Endpoints to obtain URL for asynchronous operation

tags.name: Schema
tags.description: Endpoints relating to schema retrievals and policies

tags.name: Gateway
tags.description: Endpoints regarding Gateway Provisioning Tokens (For the ['Adding External Devices' usecase](https://docs.smarthq.com/adding-external-devices/overview/))

info.description:
The Digital Twin API exists for mobile apps and other integrations to retrieve the list of devices and their state as well as send commands and subscribe to state changes.

Devices can be controlled and monitored using this REST API. See this [tutorial](https://docs.smarthq.com/device-control-and-monitoring/tutorial/) to learn how to effectively make use of this API.
info.title: SmartHQ Digital Twin API
